Title: Golgi-localized STELLO proteins regulate the assembly and trafficking of cellulose synthase complexes in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF288-containing Golgi protein with homology to CAZy GT75-related glycosyltransferases; single-pass type II membrane protein
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus; C-terminus in Golgi lumen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall; secondary cell wall; seed mucilage rays/adherent mucilage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer required for plant growth, cell elongation, secondary wall integrity and seed mucilage architecture
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose production by enabling proper cellulose synthase complex assembly, Golgi distribution, secretion and plasma-membrane activity; loss with STL2 reduces cellulose content
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to STL2; active in expanding cells and cells producing secondary cell walls
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in expanding cells and cells producing secondary cell walls
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter pSTL1 for complementation/localization; Ubiquitin10 promoter in overexpression localization constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA double mutant analysis; genetic complementation with pSTL1:STL1-GFP; cellulose quantification by Saeman hydrolysis; solid-state NMR; X-ray diffraction; subcellular localization; split-ubiquitin assay; BiFC; FRAP; BN-PAGE
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: STL2; CesA1; CesA3; CesA4; CesA6; CesA7; CesA8
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STL1 is a core Golgi-localized factor required redundantly with STL2 for normal cellulose accumulation in primary and secondary walls. 2. stl1stl2 mutants show reduced cellulose (~50% in hypocotyls, ~40% in stems), cellulose-defective growth phenotypes, collapsed xylem and defective seed mucilage. 3. STL1 co-localizes and interacts with CesA proteins and is required for proper CSC assembly, Golgi distribution, secretion and motility. 4. Point mutations in conserved DXD motifs impair STL1 function, supporting glycosyltransferase-like activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single mutants show little phenotype, indicating redundancy with STL2. Functional region is mainly the C-terminal lumenal region. Mutations DD205-206AA and DVD297-299AVA abolish complementation, whereas R271A and DD591-592AA do not. No global matrix polysaccharide composition change was detected.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated hypocotyl growth; cellulose synthesis inhibitor treatment with isoxaben or DCB
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modify cellulose deposition and cell wall biomass properties by improving CSC assembly/trafficking, though severe reduction-of-function causes major growth defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes short roots and hypocotyls, stunted growth, hypersensitivity to cellulose synthesis inhibitors, reduced secondary wall thickness and seed mucilage defects
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray co-expression analysis; solid-state NMR data repository provided in paper

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DUF288-containing Golgi protein with homology to CAZy GT75-related glycosyltransferases; single-pass type II membrane protein
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus; predicted/validated type II topology with catalytic region in Golgi lumen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Primary cell wall; secondary cell wall; seed mucilage rays/adherent mucilage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural cell wall polymer required for plant growth, cell elongation, secondary wall integrity and seed mucilage architecture
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cellulose production redundantly with STL1 by supporting cellulose synthase complex assembly and trafficking
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Similar to STL1; active in expanding cells and cells producing secondary cell walls
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in expanding cells and cells producing secondary cell walls
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA double mutant analysis; partial complementation with pUb10:STL2-GFP; cellulose quantification; localization; split-ubiquitin assay; BiFC; FRAP; BN-PAGE
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: STL1; CesA1; CesA3; CesA4; CesA6; CesA7; CesA8
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STL2 is a second STELLO protein acting redundantly with STL1 in cellulose biosynthesis-related processes. 2. Loss of STL2 together with STL1 causes broad cellulose-deficient phenotypes and reduced cellulose content. 3. STL2 localizes to the Golgi, co-localizes with CesA complexes, and interacts with CesA proteins. 4. STL2 contributes to correct CSC assembly, secretion and activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single stl2 mutants do not show strong growth defects, indicating functional redundancy. STL1 and STL2 can form homo-/heteromeric interactions.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated hypocotyl growth; cellulose synthesis inhibitor treatment with isoxaben or DCB
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for biomass/cell wall traits through CSC assembly control, but dosage must be managed to avoid growth defects
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Combined loss with STL1 causes severe cellulose-related developmental defects
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray co-expression analysis; solid-state NMR data repository provided in paper
